items.headline: Major commercial project breaks ground on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, marking significant economic activity in San José's industrial corridor. This is the largest project in this permit batch and suggests continued commercial growth in the area.

items.headline: City commissions outline 2026-27 priorities in work plan review
items.summary: Saratoga City Council met with five commissions (Heritage Preservation, Library and Public Art, Parks and Recreation, Traffic Safety, and Planning) to approve their annual work plans. The session revealed what each commission plans to focus on over the next fiscal year, including potential projects affecting parks, traffic safety, and development.

items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program and outreach to shopping centers, along with economic development incentives for retail and small businesses. This could help local business owners catch up on compliance while the city promotes its "Shop Local" campaign.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up designated as annual city-sponsored event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event, authorizing city funds and allowing the city to accept donations of materials and services. This increases municipal support for the community cleanup effort.

items.headline: City reviews federal funding applications for housing and services
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applicants for HOME and Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) federal funding on March 25. These grants support affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan to study land use and transportation changes
items.summary: The Planning Commission is beginning a study session on the San Antonio Road Area Plan, which will examine land use and transportation alternatives for this corridor. This could lead to changes in how the area develops and how traffic flows through it.

items.headline: Palo Alto moves to boost retail vitality with zoning code changes
items.summary: The Commission is considering an ordinance to amend zoning and building regulations to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. These changes are designed to support and strengthen retail districts in Palo Alto.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance, which regulates how residents can keep bees on their properties. This could affect South Bay residents interested in backyard beekeeping or concerned about bee-related policies in their neighborhood.
